item.title: Bangladesh on the brink of elimination after India loss
item.description: Bangladesh were all but out of the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup after they lost to India by five wickets at Old Trafford in Manchester on Thursday. ...

item.title: Scholastica holds A-level graduation ceremony
item.description: Scholastica concluded its two-day A-level graduation ceremony at the STM Auditorium of the school in Dhaka on Wednesday....

item.title: BUFT holds farewell for Batch 222 students
item.description: The BGMEA University of Fashion and Technology organised a farewell event for the students of Batch 222 at the BUFT in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Bangladesh committed to citizen-centric digital governance: ICT minister
item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam on Thursday highlighted Bangladesh’s future roadmap for establishing an inclusive digital public infrastructure and ensuring active citizen participation in public service delivery....

item.title: Jamaat alliance to mark uprising anniv with progs
item.description: The 11-party alliance, led by Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami, on Thursday announced a month-long programme to mark the second anniversary of the July Uprising....

item.title: Stop road construction through elephant corridor in reserved forest: GSA
item.description: The Ganosamhati Andolan has urged the authorities to halt plans to construct a road through an elephant corridor in the reserved Garjan forest at Madhushia in Chakaria upazila of Cox’s Bazar....

item.title: GAU tops Bangladesh’s public univs in THE rankings
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University secured the highest position among the public universities in Bangladesh in the Times Higher Education’s sustainability impact ratings 2026 published on Wednesday....

item.title: UGC to increase educational assistance for staff’s children
item.description: The University Grants Commission of Bangladesh has decided to increase the amount of existing merit scholarships and educational assistance to ensure education, intellectual development and higher education for the children of officials and employees....

item.title: Heatwave bakes 101m Europeans
item.description: At least 101 million Europeans were forecast to swelter in temperatures of over 35C on Thursday, as scores of people were thought to have been killed by the heatwave....

item.title: Flash flood warning issued for north
item.description: The Flood Forecasting and Warning Centre on Thursday warned that flash floods might occur in seven districts in the northern and north-eastern regions over the next seven days....

item.title: Hasnat challenges govt to probe graft allegations
item.description: National Citizen Party lawmaker Hasnat Abdullah on Thursday rejected allegations made in the Jatiya Sangsad that individuals associated with the July uprising had used their involvement in the movement for personal gain, and challenged the government to investigate him......

item.title: Fresh controversy over Shahe Alam’s bid to change rail route
item.description: Another controversy surfaced against the state minister for Local Government, Rural Development and Co-operatives, Mir Shahe Alam, as recently prime minister Tarique Rahman rejected his proposal to change the route of an ongoing railway project to......

item.title: Bangladesh, China sign 4 MoUs on media cooperation
item.description: Bangladesh and China signed four memorandums of understanding on Thursday to boost bilateral cooperation in news and information exchange....

item.title: More individuals opt for T-bonds for better returns
item.description: Individuals are increasingly investing in government treasury bills and bonds as concerns over the banking sector, stock market volatility and limited alternative investment opportunities push savers toward safer assets offering attractive returns......

item.title: PM invites Chinese cos to invest in Bangladesh
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Thursday invited Chinese companies to extend their manufacturing value chains into Bangladesh and co-author what he described as the next great Asian economic miracle, pledging full government support and investor-friendly business environment......

item.title: Bangla QR code usage must from July 1
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has announced that the usage of standardised Interoperable Quick Response (QR) code, known as ‘Bangla QR,’ will become mandatory across the country from July 1, 2026, to minimise cash transactions and transition toward a cashless economy......

item.title: Experts for stronger management at Islamic Banks
item.description: Banking experts, policymakers and academics today stressed the need for strengthening Shariah-compliant liquidity management instruments and institutional capacity to support the sustainable growth of Islamic banking in Bangladesh......

item.title: EV sector emerges as key strategy for energy security
item.description: Bangladesh’s transition toward electric mobility is taking shape as more than an environmental initiative, with the government positioning the electric vehicle (EV) sector at the centre of a broader strategy to strengthen energy security, reduce dependence on imported petroleum and promote sustainable industrialisation......

item.title: SANEM urges restructuring of energy budget allocations
item.description: The South Asian Network on Economic Modeling has urged the government to address critical allocation disparities and structural gaps in the newly proposed national budget for fiscal year FY2026-27, warning that certain provisions risk locking the country into long-term fossil fuel dependence despite ambitious green transition targets......

item.title: Govt mulls announcing shipbuilding a thrust sector: Rabiul
item.description: Shipping minister Shaikh Rabiul Alam on Wednesday said the government may consider the shipbuilding industry as a ‘thrust sector’ to increase export earnings through extensive employment generation and higher value addition......

item.title: Amazon pledges extra $13b in India AI investment
item.description: E-commerce giant Amazon announced on Thursday an additional $13-billion investment by 2030 to expand its artificial intelligence footprint in India, months after declaring plans to invest more than $35 billion......

item.title: Steps taken to enhance RMG workforce skills
item.description: The Export Promotion Bureau on Thursday signed separate agreements with the Bangladesh Garment Manufacturers and Exporters Association, the Bangladesh Knitwear Manufacturers and Exporters Association and BGMEA University of Fashion and Technology to strengthen the skills of workers and professionals in the country’s readymade garment sector......

item.title: US immigration custody deaths decade high: rights groups
item.description: The rate of people dying in Immigration and Customs Enforcement custody has reached its highest level in over a decade amid US president Donald Trump’s crackdown on immigrants, rights groups said Thursday....

item.title: Iraq urges OPEC to raise its quota
item.description: Iraq has urged OPEC to increase its oil production quota considering the country’s history of conflicts and the recent regional war that have damaged its industry, the oil ministry said Thursday......

item.title: Court orders TotalEnergies to account for clients’ emissions
item.description: A French court ordered oil and gas giant TotalEnergies on Thursday to account for the emissions from the use of its products by clients, in a high-stakes climate case brought by NGOs and the city of Paris......

item.title: Neymar returns as Ancelotti tells Brazil to stay calm
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti told Brazil fans to ​keep their feet on the ground after Neymar made his long-awaited World Cup return and Vinicius Jr ‌sparkled again as the five-times champions swept Scotland aside 3-0 on Wednesday to finish top of Group C....

item.title: Global stocks rally, oil price declines
item.description: Stock markets advanced Thursday after bumper earnings from US chip titan Micron helped calm investor worries about promised payoffs from the massive AI investment boom......

item.title: Ashura today
item.description: Ashura, the 10th day of Muharram of the Hijri year 1448, will be observed on Friday across the country....

item.title: Sonali Bank holds Shariah supervisory body meeting
item.description: Sonali Bank PLC’s Shariah supervisory committee held its 31st meeting at the bank’s head office board room on Thursday, said a press release......

item.title: NCC Banks approves 21pc dividends
item.description: National Credit and Commerce Bank PLC on Wednesday approved 17 per cent cash and 4 per cent stock dividends for the year 2025, said a press release......

item.title: BSF pushes 7 more into Bangladesh
item.description: Seven people, including six women and a child, were pushed into Bangladesh by the Indian Border Security Force through a border point under Kamalganj upazila in Moulvibazar early Thursday....

item.title: NRBC Bank holds 13th AGM
item.description: NRBC Bank on Thursday held its 13th annual general meeting in a hybrid format, said a press release......

item.title: Rupali Bank hosts innovation showcasing
item.description: Rupali Bank PLC’s innovation team on Thursday organised ‘Innovation Showcase 2025-26’ and selected the best innovative initiative, said a press release......

item.title: Prime Bank honours 7 entrepreneurs
item.description: On the occasion of World MSME Day 2026, Prime Bank PLC on Thursday honoured seven entrepreneurs from among its borrowers in recognition of their outstanding contributions to the micro, small and medium enterprises sector......

item.title: No hormuz crossings sans authorisation: Iran
item.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards on Thursday warned against any crossings of the Strait of Hormuz without authorisation, saying vessels not complying ‘will be dealt with’....

item.title: IFIC Bank, Transcom Electronics sign deal
item.description: IFIC Bank PLC and Transcom Electronics Limited have signed an agreement on Wednesday at IFIC Tower in the capital, said a press release......

item.title: Uttara Bank relocates Gulshan branch
item.description: Uttara Bank PLC on Wednesday opened new premises of its Gulshan branch, said a press release......

item.title: Prothom Alo, bKash launch quiz platform ‘Football Sigma’
item.description: On the occasion of FIFA World Cup 2026, bKash and daily Prothom Alo have launched ‘Football Sigma’, a dedicated football quiz platform, said a press release......

item.title: Xiaomi launches 3 power banks, 2 audio devices
item.description: Xiaomi has recently introduced three power banks and two audio devices, said a press release......

item.title: Bangladesh, China ink 13 memos, discuss Teesta
item.description: Bangladesh and China on Thursday agreed to strengthen cooperation on the Teesta River management project and signed 13 memorandums of understanding covering a wide range of sectors, marking a significant outcome of prime minister Tarique Rahman’s first official visit to China since assuming office....

item.title: UN refugee agency malpractice warrants accountability
item.description: THE mismanagement, irregularities and misuse of aid in several projects that the UN refugee agency runs for the Rohingyas sheltered in Bangladesh, as New Age reported on June 25, border on corruption. And, all this, therefore, entails that the United Nations should ensure accountability in these cases. The Office of Internal Oversight Services at the UN headquarters......

item.title: Govt must pay retired teachers’ benefits in six months
item.description: THE education minister’s assurance that outstanding allowances of retired non-government teachers and employees under the monthly pay order will be cleared in two years does not resolve the issue although it sounds somewhat reassuring. Thousands of teachers and employees who retired as far back as 2022 still wait for their lawful dues, with many having spent their......

item.title: Confronting the ecosystem that sustains torture
item.description: TORTURE survives not because societies openly accept it, but because they learn to live with the conditions that make it possible......

item.title: The age of strategic hedging
item.description: INTERNATIONAL politics in the twentieth century was shaped by the choices of great powers. Empires grew and collapsed; superpowers waged war; alliances rose and broke up. Smaller nations were often forced to navigate a world in which others’ choices dictated their options. Times are changing......

item.title: Why budget debate is missing the point
item.description: Every budget season follows a familiar script. Politicians argue about deficits. Economists debate revenue targets. Business leaders complain about taxes. Television talk shows obsess over growth projections. This year has been no different......

item.title: France look to do grieving Deschamps proud against Norway
item.description: France star Aurelien Tchouameni said his team were aiming to make Didier Deschamps ‘as proud as possible’ as they go into their final World Cup group game against Norway on Friday without their coach, who has returned home to attend his mother's funeral....

item.title: Pepe steers Ivory Coast into last 32 as Curacao go home
item.description: Nicolas Pepe scored twice as Ivory Coast beat Curacao 2-0 on Thursday to reach the World Cup knockout rounds for the first time ever and bring the tiny Caribbean country's run at the tournament to an end....

item.title: Ecuador stun Germany to squeeze into last 32
item.description: Ecuador kept their World Cup campaign alive as Gonzalo Plata's winner completed a memorable come-from-behind 2-1 victory over Germany to send them through to the last 32 on Thursday....

item.title: US wants deal with Iran, but not ‘at any price’
item.description: Top US diplomat Marco Rubio made the statement, warning that permitting Tehran to charge fees in the Strait of Hormuz would open the door to ‘total chaos’......

item.title: IMO suspends Hormuz evacuation after attack in Gulf of Oman
item.description: The evacuation of around 11,000 mariners stranded by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz was suspended on Thursday after an attack on a vessel in the Gulf of Oman......

item.title: Czech striker Schick ends international career
item.description: Czech striker Patrik Schick said his international career was over on Thursday, hours after the Czech Republic bowed out of the World Cup with a 3-0 loss to Mexico....

item.title: Rows, drones and 'sorry' Son as South Korea await World Cup fate
item.description: There was a row with the media, a drone buzzing a crucial training session and then captain Son Heung-min dropped by a deeply unpopular coach in a gamble that backfired....

item.title: State appoints lawyer for Pallabi rape-murder death-row couple
item.description: The government has appointed Supreme Court lawyer Hafizur Rahman Khan as state defence counsel for death-row convicts Sohel Rana and his wife Swapna Akter in the sensational Pallabi rape and murder case....

item.title: Businesses want changes in some FY27 tax measures
item.description: Businesses at a discussion on Thursday opposed some of the proposals made in the national budget for the forthcoming financial year, saying that they would hinder the pursuit of a fair and equitable taxation system....

item.title: State accountability, legal reforms to end torture sought
item.description: Human rights defenders, torture victims, civil society representatives and journalists on Thursday called for state accountability and legal reforms to end decades of systemic torture in Bangladesh....

item.title: BARI trains scientists on new tech
item.description: The training and communication wing of Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute in Gazipur concluded a 14-day training titled Newly Developed BARI Technologies for Scientists on Wednesday....

item.title: Separate ministry for haor demanded
item.description: Speakers at a press conference called for the establishment of a dedicated ministry for haor affairs to ensure sustainable development, good governance and effective coordination in the country’s wetland regions....
